artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found one primary Russian-language Economics Faculty rules PDF governing generative-AI use in that faculty's educational process, plus official Economics Faculty pages that link to it. Central MSU news was verified as official context for AI course support but not used for binding policy claims. Contest, research-display, and generic AI faculty pages were rejected or skipped as insufficient for policy claims.

artifacts.claimText: Lomonosov Moscow State University's Economics Faculty has Russian-language rules defining general conditions for use of generative AI tools by students and employees in the faculty's educational process.

citation.citationTitle: Правила применения инструментов искусственного интеллекта в учебном процессе на экономическом факультете МГУ
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The Economics Faculty rules say AI-tool use is permitted by default and that the faculty supports effective, ethical, and transparent AI use for course work, term papers, and final qualification works.

citation.citationTitle: Правила применения инструментов искусственного интеллекта в учебном процессе на экономическом факультете МГУ
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Under the Economics Faculty rules, a student who uses AI tools for work must explicitly declare that use and identify the AI model, the parts of the work where AI was used, and the purposes and methods of use.

citation.citationTitle: Правила применения инструментов искусственного интеллекта в учебном процессе на экономическом факультете МГУ
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The Economics Faculty rules place responsibility on students for correctness of facts, wording, citations, links, and interpretation of AI-assisted results, and treat unchecked use of AI results as an academic-ethics violation.

citation.citationTitle: Правила применения инструментов искусственного интеллекта в учебном процессе на экономическом факультете МГУ
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The Economics Faculty rules allow each teacher, within a discipline, to require detailed prompts or restrict AI-tool use up to a full ban, with restrictions recorded in the discipline's rating system and communicated by the first class.

citation.citationTitle: Правила применения инструментов искусственного интеллекта в учебном процессе на экономическом факультете МГУ
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The Economics Faculty rules state that violating AI-tool rules is an academic-ethics violation; if improper AI use without real mastery is found, a teacher may annul the work result and report the violation to faculty administration.

citation.citationTitle: Правила применения инструментов искусственного интеллекта в учебном процессе на экономическом факультете МГУ
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The Economics Faculty rules require teachers who use AI tools when creating materials or other work to explicitly declare that use, including the AI model, work parts, and purposes and methods of use.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Правила применения инструментов искусственного интеллекта в учебном процессе на экономическом факультете МГУ
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Если студент использует инструменты ИИ при выполнении работы, то он должен явным образом это декларировать. Для декларирования использования ИИ должны быть указать: применяемая модель ИИ; части работы, в которых применялись инструменты ИИ; цели и способы применения ИИ.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students using AI for work must explicitly declare it and identify the model, affected work parts, and purposes and methods of use.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a source-status claim limited to the Economics Faculty rules document; not phrased as a university-wide policy conclusion.
Approved because section 1.2 directly states default permission and support for effective, ethical, transparent AI use; claim remains faculty-scoped.
Approved because sections 2.1 and 2.1.1 directly require explicit declaration of student AI use and specified disclosure fields.
Approved because section 2.2.1 directly states student responsibility for facts, wording, citations, links, and interpretation of AI-assisted results.
Approved as course-level teacher authority; the claim does not infer a blanket university-wide ban or permission rule.
Approved because section 2.5 directly treats AI-rule violations as academic-ethics violations and section 2.5.2 supports the annulment/reporting consequence.
Approved because section 3.1 directly requires teacher disclosure when AI tools are used to create materials or other work.

artifacts.title: Lomonosov Moscow State University AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for Lomonosov Moscow State University uses one primary official Russian-language PDF hosted by the Economics Faculty and linked from official Economics Faculty AI-in-education pages. Approved claims are scoped to the Economics Faculty: rules scope, default permission with ethical and transparent AI use, student declaration, student responsibility, teacher course-level restrictions, academic-ethics consequences, and teacher AI disclosure. Central MSU AI-course news was verified only as context. Contest regulation, research-display, and AI faculty homepage candidates were rejected or skipped for policy specificity.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are limited to the Economics Faculty source. The crawl did not identify a university-wide Lomonosov Moscow State University generative-AI policy.
The central MSU news page is official context for AI course support, but it is not treated as binding policy evidence.
Search-result snippets were used only for discovery and were not used as evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
